name: Tarragona Roman Route
name_original: Tarragona romanica - Vuelta circular por la ciudad antigua
description: Explore Tarragona's Roman heritage on this easy 2.8-mile circular hike, passing ancient amphitheatres and forums.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Tarragones are characterized by a diverse landscape that includes coastal areas, inland mountains, and significant water features. The region features dramatic relief with limestone cliffs, ravines, and dense forests, particularly in the Prades Mountains and Serra de Montsant Natural Park. Hikers can discover picturesque water features such as Toll de L'Olla and Gorgs de la Febró, known for their natural waterfalls and pools. This varied terrain provides a range of options for exploring the natural beauty of Tarragones. Best waterfall hiking trails around Tarragones The most popular waterfall hiking route is Mas del Forès Picnic Area – Font del Glorieta loop from Ermita del Remei, a 7.4 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the region's natural water features and woodland. Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa Ana Hill and Cross – El Salt Waterfall loop from Reus, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This trail leads to El Salt Waterfall and provides varied terrain through hills and natural landscapes. Local hikers also love the El Salt Waterfall loop from Castellvell del Camp, a 2.6 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through natural woodland, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. This easy route is ideal for a shorter excursion to a waterfall. Hiking around Tarragones is defined by diverse landscapes including coastal areas, mountainous terrain with limestone formations, and lush forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Tarragones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1,500 reviews. More than 12,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Tarragones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are available in Tarragones?
A: There are over 10 dedicated waterfall hiking trails in the Tarragones region. These routes vary in difficulty and length, offering options for different experience levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go waterfall hiking in Tarragones?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Tarragones makes it suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and lush scenery. During summer, visiting waterfalls like &lt;strong&gt;Toll de L'Olla&lt;/strong&gt; or &lt;strong&gt;Gorgs de la Febró&lt;/strong&gt; can be particularly refreshing, as they often feature natural pools perfect for cooling off.

Q: Are there any easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners or families in Tarragones?
A: Yes, there are several easier options. For a shorter, more accessible experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23814325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salt Waterfall loop from Castellvell del Camp&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 4.1 km trail takes just over an hour to complete and leads through natural woodland to the waterfall.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on waterfall hikes in Tarragones?
A: Waterfall hikes in Tarragones feature diverse terrain. You can expect paths through dense forests, along limestone cliffs, and through ravines, particularly in areas like the Prades Mountains. Some trails might involve varied surfaces, from well-trodden paths to more rugged sections, especially near the waterfalls themselves.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes in Tarragones?
A: Yes, many of the waterfall trails in Tarragones are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5318156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas del Forès Picnic Area – Font del Glorieta loop from Ermita del Remei&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 12 km trail offering views of natural water features and woodlands.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about waterfall trails in Tarragones?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Tarragones, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,500 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, the refreshing natural pools found near waterfalls, and the well-marked paths that make exploration enjoyable.

Q: Are there any more challenging waterfall hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more demanding adventure, the region offers difficult routes with significant elevation gain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas del Forès Picnic Area – Font del Glorieta loop from Alcover&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 24.3 km trail that involves substantial ascents and descents through varied terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see near the waterfall trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls themselves, the Tarragones region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter impressive limestone cliffs, dense pine and oak forests, and panoramic viewpoints. Some trails are close to significant natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/477075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola (Highest Point of Tarragonès)&lt;/a&gt;, offering additional exploration opportunities.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions close to the waterfall hiking areas?
A: While the focus is on natural waterfalls, Tarragona's rich history often intertwines with its landscapes. Some hiking areas are not far from historical sites. For instance, you might find trails that pass near ancient Roman structures or charming medieval villages. The region also features attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/354543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Mèdol Roman Quarry&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: Are waterfall hikes in Tarragones generally dog-friendly?
A: Many natural areas and trails in Tarragones are welcoming to dogs, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage before heading out with your canine companion, as some protected areas might have restrictions. Ensure your dog is comfortable with the terrain and distance of your chosen waterfall hike.

Q: What should I wear or bring for a waterfall hike in Tarragones?
A: For waterfall hikes in Tarragones, it's recommended to wear comfortable hiking shoes with good grip, as paths can be uneven or wet near water features. Layers of clothing are advisable due to changing temperatures, and bringing water, snacks, and sun protection is always a good idea. If you plan to swim in natural pools, pack swimwear and a towel.
tips: Space in the center of the city of Tarragona from where you can still see the ancient forum of the Roman city. Like almost all Roman spaces in Tarragona, it is well preserved.

Tarragona Amphitheatre is a Roman amphitheatre. It was built in the 2nd century AD, sited close to the forum of this provincial capital. The amphitheatre could house up to 15,000 spectators, and measured 130 by 102 metres
Built in 73dC was the Council of the Province of Citeroris in Spain to manage Tarracus.

faqs.question: Is the Tarragona romanica - Circular tour of the ancient city suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular tour is classified as an easy hike, making it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. The paths are easily accessible and do not require any special fitness or equipment.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of easily accessible paths, including sections of asphalt, paved surfaces, streets, and footpaths. It's a well-maintained and urban-friendly route, rather than rugged natural trails.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers an immersive journey through the ancient Roman city of Tarraco, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. You'll encounter significant historical sites such as the Roman Forum of Tarragona, the impressive Tarragona Roman Amphitheatre, and the Torre del Pretorio of Tarragona. The Cathedral of Tarragona is also a notable landmark visible on the tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is accessible year-round due to its urban nature and mild Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the city's historical sites without the peak summer heat.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Tarragona romanica - Circular tour of the ancient city?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this urban route, especially on public streets and footpaths. However, they should be kept on a leash, and local regulations regarding pet access to specific historical sites or indoor areas may apply. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit to walk this circular tour through the ancient city. The route itself is free to access as it uses public paths and streets. However, individual entrance fees may apply if you wish to enter specific archaeological sites or museums along the way, such as the Roman Amphitheatre or the Cathedral.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the ancient city of Tarragona?
faqs.answer: Tarragona has several public parking options, including underground car parks, particularly around the city center and near the historical sites. Look for parking garages like Parking Saavedra, Parking Imperial Tarraco, or those near the Roman Amphitheatre for convenient access to the starting points of the tour.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trail using public transport?
faqs.answer: Tarragona is well-connected by public transport. The city's main train station (Tarragona Estació) and bus station are centrally located and within walking distance or a short local bus ride to the historical center where the tour begins. Local bus services also run frequently throughout the city.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as this is an urban route through the heart of Tarragona, you will find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops along the way, especially in the old town area. This provides ample opportunities for refreshments or a meal during or after your walk.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camí IT6TR5 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, El Serrallo, and Catalunya E25 trails, offering connections to longer routes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this circular tour?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty and urban terrain, you should bring comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) especially during warmer months. A camera is also recommended to capture the historical sights. As it's an urban route, you won't need specialized hiking gear.
